comments.author: @WildExtremeTV
comments.text:
What an amazing documentary! The cinematography, storytelling, and wildlife footage are all top-notch. Kenya’s ecosystem is both beautiful and brutal, and this film captures it perfectly.

I’m also a content creator with very few views at the moment, so videos like this motivate me to keep going. Congratulations on such an outstanding production! 👏

comments.author: @FantasiaSia-xg5kt
comments.text:
Hi,

I hope the comment finds you well.

I recently watched your documentary and truly enjoyed it. I deeply appreciate the effort your team has made producing such a wonderful video.

In China, there are many nature documentary enthusiasts who would also be interested in your work. Therefore, I would like to ask for your permission to translate the documentary into Chinese and share it with more Chinese docu-lovers.

If permitted, I plan to upload the translated version to Bilibili, a popular video-sharing platform in China. I will clearly credit your team and include the original source/link in the video description. The translated video will not be used for any commercial or profit-making purposes. You can check my Bilibili account if you consider it necessary.

Thank you very much for considering my request. I would be grateful for your permission, but I completely understand if it is not possible. I will fully respect your decision and all requirements you may have.

Best regards,
Sia

comments.author: @StephenRichman-s3q
comments.text:
The distinction between -- not just animals, but the peoples is quite peculiar OF Kenya and the ' Horn countries ' ( Somalia,  Ethiopia, and Sudan ).
The diverse majestic animals of Kenya contrast the barren lands of their neighbours just north of their border.
And the difference in body type is basically a different race.

comments.author: @StephenRichman-s3q
comments.text:
These lions look so beautiful it makes me think it's A.I.   But usually A.I. is suspect.  I would prefer its simply great quality .
The lack of authenticity in the now world is tragic.
Additionally :  I wonder if animals wonder ?  I wonder if they wonder similarly to us what is next for them.
The more we learn about our fellow creatures, the more it seems plausible.
Almost more -so since they are natural and not depraved,
as we are.
Group spirit maybe ?
A simulation cosmic conflict of sort would make most sense.
Saved from.  SAVED FOR !
